sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
MOUSE on the white dot also did nothing. Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe I need to move the black L-shape to the left side of the divider and overlap it with the charcoal L-shape. The charcoal L-shape is a left-facing L, and the white dots form a left-facing L pattern. Maybe when they overlap correctly, something happens.
Let me move the black L-shape all the way left to cols 3-11 to overlap with the charcoal L-shape.
The black L-shape is currently at cols 51-59. To get to cols 3-11, I need to move LEFT by (51-3)/3 = 16 steps. That's a lot. Let me try moving it there and see what happens.
Actually, let me first try moving LEFT a few times to see if the black L-shape can cross the divider.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move the black L-shape left to overlap with the charcoal L-shape
# Current black L at cols 51-59, charcoal at cols 3-11
# Need to move left by (51-3)/3 = 16 steps
action(['LEFT'] * 16)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After 16 LEFT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 70:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
